title: NOC 62021 – Executive Housekeepers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 62021 (Executive Housekeepers) professionals are the operational backbone of Canada’s luxury hospitality and institutional sectors. Beyond simple oversight, these TEER 2 professionals manage large-scale budgets, human resources, and environmental health standards in hotels, hospitals, and resorts. For immigration, this role is a highly reliable pathway for Permanent Residency (PR) through the Canadian Experience Class (CEC) and various Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) that prioritize the tourism and healthcare support sectors.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6202 – Service supervisorsNOC Code62021

duties: Plan and direct the activities of the housekeeping department.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate activities with other departments.
Inspect establishments to ensure high standards of cleanliness and hygiene.
Select, hire, and train new housekeeping staff.
Prepare budgets, monitor expenses, and maintain inventory of supplies.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
A university degree or college diploma in hospital or hotel management is often preferred.
Several years of experience in housekeeping or hospitality are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. For the 2022-2031 period, new job openings are expected to total approximately 8,300. The retirement of older workers and the growth of the "Senior Living" sector in Canada are primary drivers of this demand.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Executive Housekeepers is surging as Canada’s hospitality industry modernizes and healthcare facilities expand. Provinces like Alberta and Ontario have a "Good" to "Very Good" outlook. Employers are increasingly seeking international talent with specialized experience in green cleaning technologies and large-team management.
High Standards: Work in environments that adhere to world-class hygiene and safety protocols.
Managerial Growth: Direct path to Hotel Operations or Facility Management roles.
PR Stability: This NOC is frequently included in "In-Demand" lists for Atlantic Canada and the Prairies.
pathways: Express Entry (CEC): The primary route for those with 1 year of Canadian experience.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track for those working in Alberta's hospitality sector.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A key pathway for those looking to settle in the Maritimes.
